title: ajuga alba
intro: Ajuga alba, also known as White Bugle or Bugleweed, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its white or pale blue flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, possessing medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Ajuga alba include its ability to treat anxiety and insomnia, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Ajuga alba is often used as a tincture or tea, and its extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain types of cancer cells.
